canonical_definition: Based on the provided data, RoBERTa is characterized as a measurement instrument for safety evaluation, specifically for detecting harmful content. The single source paper defines it as a "harmfulness detection model" that functions as an "external safety evaluator." Operationally, it is used to assign a probability score to a given response, indicating the likelihood that the response is harmful. This work positions RoBERTa as a "state-of-the-art" tool for this purpose, employing it to assign "harmfulness probabilities to each response."
papers: AreLLMs Better than Reported? Detecting Label Errors and Mitigating Their Effect on Model Performance
EQA-RM: A Generative Embodied Reward Model with Test-time Scaling
T2: An Adaptive Test-Time Scaling Strategy for Contextual Question Answering

definitions.text: A harmfulness detection model used as an external safety evaluator to assign a probability score indicating whether a given response is harmful.
definitions.paper: EQA-RM: A Generative Embodied Reward Model with Test-time Scaling
